methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Carbyon is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Hans De Neve that has raised €22.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside a broader Climate & Energy comp universe of 8 private firms and 1 public company (LNZA).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, anchors to the median valuation of the 3-company peer set: $150M. This sample spans a range from $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte), establishing bounds around central tendency. The median reflects typical Series A valuation patterns in the sector and provides a disciplined reference point absent company-specific operational metrics or revenue data in this analysis.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $172M (high), centered on $150M. This 25% confidence interval reflects variance within the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the depth of the 3-company comparable sample, the extent to which peers align on stage definition and business model, and the absence of performance-based metrics (revenue, burn rate, unit economics) that would typically refine stage positioning. A broader analysis incorporating the full 8-private-firm universe or public market multiples would strengthen conviction.
